$schema: Optional pointer to the JSON Schema, so editors validate and autocomplete the file. `createCatalog` ignores this key.
providers: The providers the catalog can resolve, each with the models it serves.
roles: Maps stable role names your app references (`chat`, `summarize`, ...) to a provider + model pair.
providers[]: One provider and the models it serves. With no `gateway` block it is a **direct** vendor calling the bundled `@ai-sdk/*` package (its vendor is `vendor`, defaulting to `id`); adding a `gateway` block routes it through your own gateway; a provider whose vendor is not built in is wired in code via a `resolve` override.
providers[].id: Unique identifier for this provider. Becomes the model-key prefix (`openai:gpt-5.6`) and the default vendor. Must not contain `:` (it would make the `"provider:model"` role shorthand ambiguous).
providers[].vendor: The bundled `@ai-sdk/*` vendor backing this **direct** provider: either the vendor id as a string shorthand (see `providers[].vendor.id` for the allowed values), or a block that also overrides the endpoint, key, headers, and query. Defaults to `id`. Not allowed alongside `gateway`.
providers[].vendor.id: The bundled vendor. Defaults to the provider's `id`.
providers[].vendor.baseURL: Custom endpoint for the vendor — a proxy, or an OpenAI-compatible server such as Ollama. Required when the vendor is `openai-compatible`.
providers[].vendor.apiKey: API key: a literal string, or `{ "envVarName": "..." }` to read it from that environment variable at first use. Omit to use the vendor SDK's own default (e.g. `OPENAI_API_KEY`).
providers[].vendor.apiKey.envVarName: Name of the environment variable to read the value from.
providers[].vendor.name: Metadata namespace for the `openai-compatible` vendor.
providers[].vendor.headers: Extra request headers sent with every request, merged over the vendor SDK's own (a same-name header overrides the SDK's — e.g. an explicit `x-api-key`). A value is either a literal string, in which `{apiKey}` is replaced with the resolved API key, or `{ "envVarName": "..." }` to read it from an environment variable at first use.
providers[].vendor.query: Query parameters appended to every request URL, e.g. `"api-version": "2026-01-01"`. A parameter already present in the URL is overridden. Values are plain text — don't put secrets in URLs; use a header instead.
providers[].gateway: Describes your own LLM gateway: where it lives, its key, and how each upstream backend is laid out on it. The presence of this block is what makes the provider gateway-routed.
providers[].settings: Default AI SDK call settings inherited by every model of this provider. They are baked into the model handle, and can still be overridden per call.
providers[].models[].id: The vendor's model id (e.g. `gpt-5.6`). Referenced by `roles` and used in the `provider:model` key.
providers[].models[].api: The API surface used to call the model. Omit for the vendor's default: the Responses API for OpenAI, Chat Completions for an OpenAI-compatible server, and the single native surface for every other vendor.
providers[].models[].backend: Gateway providers only: the key in `gateway.backends` that serves this model.
providers[].models[].slug: Gateway providers only: the value substituted for `{slug}` in the backend's `pathTemplate`, when it differs from `id`.
providers[].models[].settings: Default call settings for this model, merged over the provider's `settings`: scalar fields are overridden by the model, and `providerOptions` are merged per provider namespace.
providers[].gateway.baseURL: Base URL of the gateway, e.g. `https://gateway.example.com/v1`.
providers[].gateway.apiKey: API key for the gateway: a literal string, or `{ "envVarName": "..." }`. Omit to read the `AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY` environment variable.
providers[].gateway.headers: Extra request headers sent with every request to the gateway (all backends). Same value forms as `providers[].vendor.headers`: a literal string in which `{apiKey}` is replaced with the gateway key (e.g. `"Bearer {apiKey}"`), or `{ "envVarName": "..." }` read from the environment at first use.
providers[].gateway.query: Query parameters appended to every request URL, after the gateway path rewriting — e.g. a mandatory `"api-version": "2026-01-01"`.
providers[].gateway.backends: How each upstream backend is laid out on the gateway, under keys of your choice — so the same vendor can appear more than once (e.g. two regions). Each model picks its backend by key via `backend`.
providers[].gateway.backends.*: One upstream backend: the vendor it speaks and its layout on the gateway.
providers[].gateway.backends.*.vendor: The upstream vendor this backend speaks.
providers[].gateway.backends.*.pathTempl: Path appended to the gateway's `baseURL`. Must contain `{slug}`, replaced per request with the model's slug (e.g. `anthropic/{slug}`); for every vendor except `google` the model also travels in the request body, so the path is fixed per backend. For a `google` backend the model lives in the URL, so the template must also contain `{action}` (e.g. `google/{slug}:{action}`). A region is just a path segment you write in.
providers[].gateway.backends.*.actionMap: `google` only: renames Google's action segment per method when the gateway uses different names (e.g. `streamGenerateContent` → `customStreamGenerateContent`).
providers[].gateway.backends.*.name: `openai-compatible` only: provider name used for metadata namespacing. Defaults to `openai-compatible`.
providers[].gateway.backends.*.headers: Extra headers for this backend only, merged over the gateway-level `headers` (backend wins per name).
providers[].gateway.backends.*.query: Query parameters for this backend only, merged over the gateway-level `query` (backend wins per name).
providers[].settings.maxOutputTokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate.
providers[].settings.temperature: Sampling temperature. The meaningful range depends on the provider and model.
providers[].settings.topP: Nucleus sampling. It is recommended to set either `temperature` or `topP`, not both.
providers[].settings.topK: Only sample from the top K options for each subsequent token.
providers[].settings.presencePenalty: Penalty for the model repeating information already present in the prompt.
providers[].settings.frequencyPenalty: Penalty for the model repeatedly using the same words or phrases.
providers[].settings.stopSequences: Sequences that stop text generation when produced.
providers[].settings.seed: Seed for deterministic sampling, where the model supports it.
providers[].settings.providerOptions: Provider-specific options passed through untouched, keyed by provider namespace (e.g. `"openai": { "reasoningEffort": "low" }`). Values must be plain JSON.
roles.*: One role assignment; the map key is the role name your app uses. Either the shorthand string `"provider:model"` (split at the first `:`, so model ids may contain colons), or an object `{ provider, model }`. Must reference a provider and model declared in `providers`.
roles.*.provider: The `id` of a provider declared in `providers`.
roles.*.model: The `id` of a model served by that provider.
